audit_items.proof_trace: Two call sites in tool_args() assign ber_strdup() directly into passwd.bv_val and immediately call strlen(passwd.bv_val) with no intervening NULL check (L982→L990, L1476→L1477). ber_strdup returns NULL when ber_memalloc_x fails (OOM), making both strlen calls a confirmed NULL dereference path. No other enforcement mechanism exists in the function to guard these uses.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If the system is under memory pressure when a user runs any OpenLDAP client tool with -w or interactive password prompt (-W), ber_strdup() returns NULL and strlen(passwd.bv_val) dereferences it, crashing the client process. An attacker who can cause memory exhaustion on the client host (e.g., through a co-located process or resource-limit manipulation) can reliably crash any LDAP tool invocation that uses password authentication.
